items.question: Who typically sets up MLflow or W&B in a machine learning team?
items.answer: ML engineers and MLOps practitioners wire tracking into training scripts and CI; data scientists log runs daily; platform teams host MLflow tracking servers or manage W&B org/project access.

items.question: What is logged on an experiment run in MLflow or W&B?
items.answer: A unique run ID plus parameters (hyperparameters, seed, data version), metrics (scalar scores often per step/epoch), and artifacts (model files, plots, configs) stored under that run for search and comparison.

items.question: When should you call log_param vs log_metric?
items.answer: Log params once at run start for static config (learning_rate, architecture, seed); log metrics repeatedly during training for values that change (train_loss, val_f1) using step or epoch for time series.

items.question: Where does MLflow store run data by default?
items.answer: Locally under `./mlruns` when using the default file-based tracking URI unless you set `mlflow.set_tracking_uri` or `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` to a remote HTTP tracking server or Databricks workspace.

items.question: Why compare runs on validation metrics instead of training metrics?
items.answer: Training metrics reflect fit on seen data; validation metrics approximate generalization on held-out data. Comparing runs on val scores avoids picking an overfit config — split design is in Train/Val/Test Splits.

items.question: How do you compare three runs in the MLflow UI?
items.answer: Open the experiment, checkbox the runs, click Compare — the table shows params and metrics side by side. Programmatically use MlflowClient.search_runs with order_by on metrics.val_auc DESC.

items.question: MLflow vs W&B — hosting and default workflow?
items.answer: MLflow defaults to local `mlruns` and integrates with self-hosted tracking servers and Databricks; W&B defaults to cloud projects with team dashboards. MLflow suits OSS-first pipelines; W&B suits live collaboration and rich visual comparison out of the box.

items.question: MLflow log_param vs W&B config — equivalent usage?
items.answer: Both capture hyperparameters: MLflow uses discrete `log_param(key, value)` calls; W&B uses `wandb.config` or `config=` in `init`. W&B config supports nested dicts in the UI; MLflow params are flat string key-value pairs per run.

items.question: MLflow artifacts vs W&B artifacts — retrieval?
items.answer: MLflow artifacts live under `runs:/<run_id>/path` URIs and download via client or UI; W&B artifacts are versioned objects with `use_artifact` for lineage. Both attach files to a run; MLflow couples tightly to Model Registry paths.

items.question: What if two runs use different metric key names for the same score?
items.answer: Comparison tables show empty cells or misleading sorts — run A's `val_f1` cannot rank against run B's `f1_validation`. Standardize keys before sweeping and enforce them in a shared logging helper.

items.question: What if you forget to log seed and data_version on half the runs?
items.answer: You cannot tell whether a higher val_auc came from better hyperparameters or a luckier split — comparisons become inconclusive. Re-run or tag those runs as incomplete rather than promoting a winner.

items.question: Cause: Log val_auc with step=epoch on every run in a sweep. Effect?
items.answer: MLflow and W&B store metric time series so you can compare learning curves, spot early stopping opportunities, and detect overfitting when train metrics improve but val metrics plateau.

items.question: Cause: Set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to a shared team server. Effect?
items.answer: All teammates' runs land in one searchable store — compare runs across machines and CI jobs by experiment name instead of copying local `mlruns` folders.
